findingVerification.notes: The benchmark warden.toml explicitly sets defaults.verification.enabled = true.

traceCapture.notes: Run used --traces on all six clean shards. Raw JSONL artifacts are withheld pending sensitive-data review; committed traceSummaries retain sanitized per-chunk metadata only.

timing.notes: macOS entered idle sleep at 2026-07-10 16:39:27 -0400 during the 9fc06de5 shard and did not return to full wake before the benchmark completed. The observed full-run timing and final two shards are not comparable provider-latency measurements.

cleanPreSleepAnalysisChunkMs.source: The first four shards completed before sustained host sleep.

scores.notes: Internal EA features endpoint accessible to any authenticated user
UserInfo accepts tokens for inactive users and OAuth apps
Empty project list drops scope and lists all orgs' release thresholds
Cleartext option-value logging is real but is not the allowlist tuple corpus bug at this commit.
Signed-cookie credential persistence is not the SUPERUSER_ORG_ID fail-open corpus bug.
SSO reauth skipping the U2F challenge is not the SUPERUSER_ORG_ID fail-open corpus bug.
Existing members can delete arbitrary invites without a valid token
OAuth client_secret exposed to non-owner via nested authorization serializer
CLIENT_SECRET_JWT allows replay after 60s and accepts non-expiring tokens
Project rule details resolves workflows without project scope
Timing-unsafe comparisons are not the Statsig timestamp freshness corpus bug.
Bitbucket Server webhook processes forged events without authenticity check
Spoofable forwarded-IP fallback is not the invalid-signature HMAC logging corpus bug.
Valid Jira Server webhook JWT credential written to application logs
Atlassian Connect asymmetric JWT does not pin algorithm to RS256 or validate CDN key response
Missing project-level authorization on size analysis download
Issue-link request SSRF is not the missing event-scope corpus bug.
Org-level Seer RPC methods skip project membership checks
Mailgun inbound webhook lacks timestamp/token replay protection
Anomaly data endpoint skips project-level authorization
JQL injection in search_issues via incomplete quote escaping
Unescaped method and header names in getCurlCommand allow shell injection when copied
Unescaped feature flag name enables XSS in chart tooltip
Stored XSS via unescaped seriesName in chart tooltip HTML
App size treemap tooltip XSS via unescaped file names/paths
Pinning search updates any position-0 starred GroupSearchView without ownership check
Any org member can regenerate Codecov upload tokens
Control SSO owner override excludes wrong column (mapping PK vs organizationmember_id)
External team update/delete allows cross-team IDOR within org
Perforce password/ticket returned unredacted via configData API
Perforce install-probe SSRF is not the configData password exposure corpus bug.
MS Teams issue actions skip org-integration binding check
Slack response_url logging is not either same-file authorization corpus bug.
Approve endpoint skips project-level authorization
Pub/Sub retry webhook lacks push-identity verification, letting any authenticated user re-run transfer jobs
continue_run accepts any run_id without group ownership check
Issue-link request SSRF is not the GroupEventJsonView project-access corpus bug.
Merge verification code expiry (is_valid) never enforced
create_external_issue allows mutations without project access
Workflow update can disconnect detectors without project alerts permission
PUT logs full option values including credentials

scoring.notes: Agent-verified semantic matches. A finding counts when it identifies the same bug in roughly the same location as an existing corpus finding. Same-file findings about different bugs do not count; duplicate findings map to one unique corpus ID.

notes: Full benchmark result stitched from one clean Warden run per corpus SHA using --runtime pi --model openrouter/x-ai/grok-4.5 --effort high --traces. Each shard analyzes every unique corpus path for that SHA, not the whole Sentry repository. All 156 chunks completed successfully with complete traces and no repair artifacts. macOS sleep logs show that the host entered sustained idle sleep during the fifth shard and remained in sleep or dark wake through the final shard, so the observed 217.5-minute total and final-shard timing are contaminated. The first four pre-sleep shards covered 90 chunks with a 62.3-second P50, 3.4-minute P90, and 5.4-minute maximum. The run emitted 41 verified findings and spent substantial auxiliary verification cost because of that review volume. Raw JSONL artifacts are withheld pending sensitive-data review.
